title: psoralea latifolia
intro: Psoralea latifolia, also known as the goat's thorn or wide-leaved psoralea, is a plant species native to Australia and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote the breakdown of radioactive iodine in the body. The health benefits of Psoralea latifolia include its potential to improve thyroid function, support bone health, and act as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
